prompt: (Scarcely) he see his opponent than he went numb with fear.
reason: Option (a) is correct. We use 'than' with 'No sooner'. Since 'than' is in the later part of the sentence, 'No sooner did' should be used.

prompt: No sooner had I reached the station (when the train started moving.)
options: when the train had started moving
then the train had started moving

prompt: No sooner had the sun risen over the horizon (when the birds began) to chirp and the squirrels started to scamper around the park.
(No sooner did he finished) his examination than he left for his cricket training centre.
options: No sooner than he did finished
reason: Option (a) is correct. The first form of the verb (V1) is used with 'did', so 'finish' will replace 'finished'.

prompt: No sooner did Priya/get her report card/when she started jumping/with joy.
reason: Option (d) is correct (contains error). Replace 'when' with 'than'.
